acceptance_targets: [ch01-award-001] PASS if, on a fresh read of the sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:239, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:269, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:289, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:339, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:365, each surviving exchange changes the relationship in a distinct way and no two consecutive exchanges end on the same beat of Lena simply smiling/laughing and feeling more interested. FAIL if adjacent exchanges still do no new work beyond 'Eli seems particular and she likes it.'

[ch01-prose-001] PASS if no more than two reaction beats across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:311, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:345, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:383, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:415 use the construction 'looked at the' or 'stared at the' plus phone/message/screen, and each remaining beat delivers a different bodily or sensory reaction. FAIL if the sequence still stages most of Lena's responses by having her simply look at the device.

[ch01-prose-002] PASS if the phrase 'in a way' appears no more than once across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:237, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:273, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:425, and each revised sentence still lands a concrete image or social inference. FAIL if the same qualifying template still appears three or more times in those positions.

[FA-012] Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:219 still renders June's opacity through concrete detail and chapter 1 does not add a second facial label from the same family nearby. Fail if the moment becomes more generic or starts doubling the shorthand.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, on a fresh read of the sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:239, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:269, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:289, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:339, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:365, each surviving exchange changes the relationship in a distinct way and no two consecutive exchanges end on the same beat of Lena simply smiling/laughing and feeling more interested. FAIL if adjacent exchanges still do no new work beyond 'Eli seems particular and she likes it.'
findings.problem: The middle Hinge section keeps proving the same thing: Eli feels unusually particular and Lena is newly disarmed by it. Because several adjacent exchanges land on the same pleasant-surprise beat, the chapter sheds some of the tax/work pressure and loses the harder compression an award-level opening needs.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten and differentiate the exchange sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:239, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:269, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:289, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:339, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:365. Keep one beat that proves Eli listens, one beat that exposes Lena's taste or history, one beat that shows concrete work-cost, and one beat that earns the voice-note threshold; cut or reframe any turn that only restates that he is charming.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two reaction beats across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:311, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:345, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:383, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:415 use the construction 'looked at the' or 'stared at the' plus phone/message/screen, and each remaining beat delivers a different bodily or sensory reaction. FAIL if the sequence still stages most of Lena's responses by having her simply look at the device.
findings.problem: The back half overuses the same reaction setup, repeatedly staging Lena's escalating interest through 'looked at the ...' beats. Five nearly identical eye-to-screen moves flatten the rhythm just when the chapter should feel more physically involuntary.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the reaction beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:311, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:345, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:383, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:415. Replace at least three of those visual check-ins with distinct bodily or spatial responses tied to pressure: abandoned keystrokes, thumb hover, window movement, heat in the phone, shifting posture on the bed, or sound hitting her body before thought does.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the phrase 'in a way' appears no more than once across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:237, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:273, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:425, and each revised sentence still lands a concrete image or social inference. FAIL if the same qualifying template still appears three or more times in those positions.
findings.problem: The phrase-template 'in a way ...' recurs four times across description and interior response. In a chapter whose strength is hard specificity, that repeated qualification starts to feel like hedging rather than precision.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:237, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:273, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:425, rewrite at least three of the 'in a way' clauses into direct image or judgment. Let the sentence make the comparison outright instead of cushioning it with a repeated qualifier.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:219 still renders June's opacity through concrete detail and chapter 1 does not add a second facial label from the same family nearby. Fail if the moment becomes more generic or starts doubling the shorthand.
findings.problem: The novel defaults to labeling June unreadable or blank in 9 chapters. This first instance is the cleanest and is the one the book should keep, but it needs to carry the job without spawning echoes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the description at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:219 as the book's canonical early unreadability beat, but if you revise it, sharpen the concrete tiredness, work, and nicotine particulars rather than adding another synonym in this chapter. This should be the retained seed, not the start of a repeated label.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
